title: Boat Rental in San Diego: Prices, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
With its sheltered bay and year-round sunshine, San Diego is one of America's finest boating destinations. Whether you depart from Shelter Island or Coronado, expect to pay between $250 and $3,000 for a half- or full-day rental. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners so you can compare boats and book with confidence.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in San Diego?

In 2026, expect to pay from $250 for a shared tour to over $3,000 for a crewed luxury yacht. Prices vary by boat type, season and duration. A half-day on a small motorboat starts around $400, while a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests averages $1,500. To find the best deal, browse verified options through boat hire San Diego on BoatHire24, where you can filter by price, size and amenities.

Where to set off in San Diego

San Diego Bay offers multiple launching points, each with its own character. Shelter Island Marina provides calm waters and easy parking. Down the coast, Coronado Ferry Landing gives quick access to the open ocean. Further south, Chula Vista Marina is a quieter base, while Mission Bay, a man-made aquatic park, is perfect for relaxed cruising. For offshore trips, head to Point Loma or the nearby town of La Jolla, known for sea caves and seals.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (e.g. Axopar 24), best for couples or small groups wanting speed and agility; sleeps 4; from $400 for half-day.
  2. Bowrider or deck boat, ideal for families and watersports; up to 8 guests; from $450 for half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), stable and spacious for parties or sunset cruises; up to 12 guests; from $800 for half-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Princess F70), fully crewed with flybridge and saloon; up to 10 guests; from $2,500 for full-day.
  5. Classic sailing yacht, for a quiet, wind-powered experience; up to 6 guests; from $600 for half-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in San Diego?

California does not require a licence for bareboat rentals of small motorboats, but you must be at least 18 and complete a safety checklist. For larger vessels or crewed charters, the operator provides a captain. The boat owner or hire platform confirms exact requirements at booking.

If you are planning a day trip from Shelter Island, check the wind forecast: the Santa Ana winds in autumn can create choppy conditions, especially near Point Loma. Your skipper will know the best sheltered coves.

At a glance

  • Price range: $250, $3,000 for half- or full-day
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours (half-day) to 8 hours (full-day)
  • Maximum guests: 12 on most vessels
  • Currency: USD

Cost breakdown

  • Shared tour (2 hours): from $250 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hrs): from $400
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hrs): from $1,500
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (8 hrs): from $3,000

Questions about boat rental in San Diego

Can I rent a boat without experience in San Diego?

Yes, many boats come with a captain, so no previous experience is necessary; just bring your group and enjoy the view.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in San Diego?

The prime season runs from May to September, when ocean temperatures are warm and the sea breeze is reliable, though year-round boating is possible.

How far can I travel from San Diego on a half-day rental?

A half-day cruise of four hours can cover up to 20 nautical miles, enough to reach La Jolla or the Coronado Islands with a fast motorboat.

Are pets allowed on rental boats in San Diego?

Policies vary; check the owner’s rules on the BoatHire24 listing before booking, as some vessels welcome well-behaved dogs.

Planning a day on the water in San Diego is straightforward when you use BoatHire24 to compare boats, read reviews and book directly with owners. To explore options, visit the the Prestige 500 listing for a luxury day charter. If you own a boat and want to earn extra income, consider listing your vessel on the platform to reach renters worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in San Diego and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
